coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Wednesday = vert day in peak phase. Hit 4,000–5,000 feet of climbing over 90–110 minutes at Z3–Z4 intensity. Target sustained efforts on steep pitches (8–12% grade) where you'll practice the cadence and breathing rhythm you'll need in the Julian Alps' relentless ascents. Focus on grinding repeatable power, not speed; the 50K will demand exactly this—long, steep climbs with sparse aid. If you're traveling, find a hill repeats loop or stairwell; 8–10 × 8-minute climbs works if terrain is limited.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're 107 days out and in peak load—don't skimp. Eat 200–300g carbs the night before (pasta, rice, oats), and fuel 2 hours pre-run with 60g carbs + 15g protein. During the vert session, if it runs over 90 minutes, take 30–40g carbs every 45 minutes (energy chew, sports drink, or gel). Post-run window: 50g carbs + 25g protein within 30 minutes to arrest muscle breakdown from the climbing stress. Your travel schedule is brutal; prep breakfast the night before or buy it the morning of—no excuses on fueling during peak.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You're in week 19 of a 22-week block and not in a recovery week, so sleep is non-negotiable. Aim for 8 hours minimum, 8.5 if possible—peak training + executive travel + time zone shifts compound fatigue. Tomorrow (Thursday) is easy recovery, so use today's evening to foam roll the quads, hip flexors, and calves for 12 minutes post-run while hydrating. No alcohol tonight; you're burning through glycogen and need full restoration. If legs feel heavy, 10 minutes of easy spinning (Z1) in the evening clears metabolic junk better than sitting.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 15 weeks from Julian Alps. This vert session is a dress rehearsal for the alpine grind—use it to build confidence in sustained climbing power, not fear. Notice your breathing pattern, where your mind checks out, and how you self-talk on the steep sections. In a 50K with 2,500m of vert, you'll repeat these mental moments 5–6 times. Lean into the discomfort now. Also: scout the actual race course online (Strava, race maps) today or this weekend; knowing the terrain and aid station locations kills anxiety on race day.
